title: cerastium pentandrum subsp. gracile
intro: Cerastium pentandrum subsp. gracile, also known as a type of wheatflower or lesser mouse-ear, is a plant species belonging to the carnation family. Traditionally, the leaves and stems of this plant have been used to treat various ailments, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cerastium pentandrum subsp. gracile include its potential to soothe skin irritations and reduce swelling, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions or decoctions of the leaves and stems, which can be consumed as teas or applied topically to the affected areas.
